overview.tagline: Bergen County's Aviation and Industrial Hub
overview.description: Teterboro is one of the smallest municipalities in Bergen County and all of New Jersey, distinguished by its almost entirely commercial and industrial character. The borough is best known as home to Teterboro Airport, a general aviation reliever airport for the New York metropolitan area that primarily serves private and corporate aircraft. With a resident population of fewer than 100 people, Teterboro is a unique municipality where the daytime working population vastly exceeds its residents. The borough generates substantial tax revenue from its commercial base, airport operations, and industrial properties.
overview.schoolDistrict: Teterboro Public Schools (sends students to neighboring districts)
highlights: Home to Teterboro Airport, a premier general aviation airport
One of the smallest boroughs by population in New Jersey
Almost entirely commercial and industrial land use
Aviation Hall of Fame & Museum of New Jersey located here
Strategic location at junction of Routes 17, 46, and I-80

realEstate.assessmentInfo: Due to its almost entirely commercial/industrial tax base, Teterboro has one of the lowest residential property tax rates in New Jersey. Very few residential properties exist.
realEstate.recentTrends: Residential real estate in Teterboro is extremely limited. The borough's real estate market is dominated by commercial and industrial properties, including warehouse, logistics, and aviation-related facilities. There is virtually no residential development activity.
neighborhoodNotes: The small residential area is located on the eastern side of the borough
Most of the borough consists of airport property, warehouses, and commercial buildings
The Meadowlands environment borders the borough to the south and west
Adjacent to Hasbrouck Heights, Moonachie, and South Hackensack

trashCollection.title: Trash Collection
trashCollection.description: Trash collection is provided for the small number of residential properties in the borough.
details: Collection provided by a contracted hauler
Limited residential service due to the very small resident population
Commercial properties arrange private waste removal
Contact Borough Hall for residential collection schedule

recycling.title: Recycling
recycling.description: Recycling services are coordinated through the Bergen County Utilities Authority for the limited residential properties.
details: Curbside recycling available for residents
Standard Bergen County recycling guidelines apply
Commercial properties must arrange private recycling services
BCUA provides guidance on accepted materials

waterSewer.title: Water & Sewer
waterSewer.description: Water and sewer services are provided to the borough's properties, with sewer treatment handled by the Bergen County Utilities Authority.
details: Water supplied through a regional utility provider
Sewer treated by Bergen County Utilities Authority
Commercial and industrial users account for the vast majority of water/sewer usage
Contact Borough Hall for service information

permits.title: Building & Zoning Permits
permits.description: The borough handles building permits and zoning for its predominantly commercial and industrial properties.
details: Permits required for construction and renovation of commercial and industrial properties
Zoning is predominantly industrial and commercial
Airport-related development may require FAA coordination
Contact the Building Department at Borough Hall for applications

parking.title: Parking
parking.description: Parking in Teterboro is primarily managed by individual commercial properties and the airport.
details: No municipal metered parking
Airport parking managed by individual FBOs (Fixed Base Operators)
Commercial properties provide their own parking facilities
Street parking is extremely limited

additionalServices.title: Airport Services
additionalServices.description: Teterboro Airport provides general aviation services through multiple Fixed Base Operators.
details: Jet Aviation, Meridian, and Signature Flight Support operate as FBOs
Customs and Border Protection facility on site
Noise abatement procedures in effect
Airport managed by the Port Authority of New York and New Jersey

noise.title: Noise Ordinance
noise.summary: Teterboro has noise regulations that apply to both ground-level activity and aircraft operations, though aircraft noise is primarily regulated at the federal level.
keyRules: Standard quiet hours apply for ground-level noise
Aircraft noise abatement procedures are managed by the Port Authority and FAA
Teterboro Airport has a voluntary nighttime curfew (midnight to 6:00 AM) for most aircraft
Stage 1 and Stage 2 aircraft are prohibited
Noise complaints related to aircraft can be filed with the Port Authority
noise.source: Borough of Teterboro Code; FAA/Port Authority Airport Rules

propertyMaintenance.title: Property Maintenance
propertyMaintenance.summary: Commercial and industrial property owners must maintain their properties in accordance with borough codes.
keyRules: Properties must be maintained in good repair
Landscaping and exterior areas must be kept neat and orderly
No accumulation of debris or abandoned materials
Signage must comply with borough zoning regulations
Environmental regulations apply due to Meadowlands proximity

pets.title: Pet & Animal Regulations
pets.summary: Standard pet licensing and leash requirements apply, though the very small residential population means minimal enforcement activity.
keyRules: Dogs must be licensed and vaccinated against rabies
Dogs must be on a leash when off the owner's property
Pet waste must be cleaned up
Standard Bergen County animal control regulations apply

parking.title: Parking Regulations
parking.summary: Parking regulations are oriented toward commercial and industrial traffic management.
keyRules: No parking on borough streets without authorization
Commercial vehicle parking must be on designated properties
No overnight parking on public streets
Truck routes are designated through the borough

rentals.title: Rental Property Regulations
rentals.summary: Very limited rental housing exists in Teterboro. Standard housing codes apply to any residential rental units.
keyRules: Rental units must meet all housing code standards
Residential zoning is extremely limited
Commercial leases govern the vast majority of rental activity in the borough
Contact Borough Hall for specific residential rental questions

other.title: Airport Zoning Restrictions
other.summary: Height and land use restrictions apply in areas surrounding Teterboro Airport to ensure aviation safety.
keyRules: Building heights are restricted within airport approach zones
FAA Part 77 obstruction standards apply
Certain land uses incompatible with airport operations are prohibited
New construction near the airport requires FAA review (Form 7460)
Bird and wildlife attractant uses may be restricted near the airport
other.source: FAA Regulations; Borough of Teterboro Zoning Code

other.title: Meadowlands Environmental Regulations
other.summary: Parts of Teterboro fall within the New Jersey Meadowlands District, subject to additional environmental oversight.
keyRules: Development may require permits from the New Jersey Sports and Exposition Authority / Meadowlands Commission
Wetlands protections apply to undeveloped areas
Stormwater management requirements are strict
Environmental impact assessments may be required for major projects

publicSchools.name: Teterboro Public School (sends students to neighboring districts)
publicSchools.notes: Teterboro does not operate its own school buildings due to its tiny population. Students are sent to neighboring school districts (primarily Hasbrouck Heights) through send-receive agreements.

whyMoveHere: Extremely low property taxes due to massive commercial/industrial tax base
Strategic location near major highways (Routes 17, 46, I-80)
Close to employment centers in the Meadowlands area

commuteToNYC.notes: Teterboro itself has minimal public transit. Residents typically drive to nearby NJ Transit bus stops in Hasbrouck Heights or Hackensack for bus service to Manhattan. The Lincoln Tunnel and George Washington Bridge are accessible via Routes 17 and 46. The Meadowlands area has several NJ Transit bus connections.

costOfLiving.summary: Teterboro offers an unusually low cost of living for Bergen County due to its heavily commercial tax base subsidizing the tiny residential population. Property taxes are a fraction of what neighboring towns charge.
costOfLiving.vsStateAverage: Property taxes approximately 70% below the Bergen County average
costOfLiving.vsNationalAverage: Housing costs are moderate; property taxes are well below national average

pros: Extremely low property taxes — among the lowest in Bergen County
Close to shopping, dining, and employment in surrounding towns
Small community where everyone knows each other
cons: No local schools — students sent to neighboring districts
Airport and industrial noise is a constant factor
No walkable downtown, shops, or restaurants within the borough
Industrial/commercial environment lacks typical suburban charm
Flood risk in some low-lying Meadowlands areas

restaurants.name: The Hasbrouck Heights Diner
restaurants.description: Classic New Jersey diner just across the border in Hasbrouck Heights, serving breakfast, lunch, and dinner with a wide-ranging menu.

restaurants.name: Ivy Inn
restaurants.description: Charming restaurant and bar in nearby Hasbrouck Heights known for its American cuisine and warm atmosphere.

restaurants.name: Baci Ristorante
restaurants.description: Italian restaurant near Teterboro Airport popular with business travelers and locals, offering traditional Italian dishes.

parks.name: Losen Slote Creek Park
parks.description: A small nature area along Losen Slote Creek in the Meadowlands, offering a glimpse of the wetland ecosystem. Located partly in the Teterboro/Little Ferry area.

parks.name: Hasbrouck Heights Municipal Parks
parks.description: Several parks in neighboring Hasbrouck Heights are the closest recreational facilities, including athletic fields and playgrounds.

parks.name: DeKorte Park (Meadowlands Environment Center)
parks.description: Part of the NJ Meadowlands, this park in nearby Lyndhurst offers walking trails through marshlands, birdwatching, and the Meadowlands Environment Center.

events.name: Aviation Hall of Fame Induction Ceremony
events.description: Annual ceremony honoring inductees into the New Jersey Aviation Hall of Fame, held at the museum on the airport grounds.

events.name: Airport Open House / Aviation Events
events.description: Periodic events at Teterboro Airport or hosted by FBOs, featuring aircraft displays and aviation-related activities.

events.name: Meadowlands Birding Events
events.description: Birdwatching and nature events in the Meadowlands area, including the area around Teterboro, organized by environmental groups.

attractions.name: Aviation Hall of Fame & Museum of New Jersey
attractions.description: Museum dedicated to the history of aviation in New Jersey, featuring exhibits on famous aviators, vintage aircraft, and flight simulators. Located on the grounds of Teterboro Airport.

attractions.name: Teterboro Airport
attractions.description: One of the busiest general aviation airports in the United States, managed by the Port Authority of NY & NJ. A fascinating spot for aviation enthusiasts to watch private jets and corporate aircraft.

government.meetingSchedule: Regular meetings are held on the 3rd Wednesday of each month at 7:00 PM
government.meetingLocation: Teterboro Borough Hall, 510 Route 46 West, Teterboro, NJ 07608

fire.address: Moonachie Fire Department provides fire coverage
